urn:contentItem:6JK7-7K23-S048-H203-00000-00MCCARTHY BUILDING COMPANIES, INC. - COMPLETE UPDATES ON INDUSTRY AND PEER NEWS2026-05-14T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:40:40ZLexisNexisSTOCKMCCARTHY BUILDING COMPANIES, INC. - COMPLETE UPDATES ON INDUSTRY AND PEER NEWS

DASHBOARD: MCCARTHY HOLDINGS

PermID4296686586Websitehttp://www.mccarthy.com/IndustryConstruction & EngineeringAddress1604 Eastport Plaza Drive, Suite 106 (Map it) Collinsville, Illinois 62234

ACTIVITIES:

McCarthy Building Companies, Inc., founded in 1864, is an employee-owned construction firm headquartered in St. Louis, Missouri. With over 7,000 employees, it provides preconstruction and construction services for healthcare, commercial, and renewable energy projects, generating $5.5 billion in 2023 revenue. Notable projects include the Mayo Clinic expansion. McCarthys focus on safety, quality, and technology, including BIM, ensures efficient project delivery. Serving clients like Kaiser Permanente, it competes with Skanska while fostering a collaborative culture and sustainability initiatives, maintaining a strong presence in the U.S. construction market.

+ MCCARTHY HOLDINGS [3 News That Matters]

+ AUDDIA AND THRAMANN CREATE MCCARTHY FINNEY, INC. IN STRATEGIC MERGER

Post-merger ownership is projected to be about 20% for Auddia and 80% for Thramann, subject to a minimum cash balance of $12 million at closing. The special preferred stock issued to Thramann carries a liquidation preference of at least $20.5 million and features flexible conversion and redemption terms, affording Thramann substantial governance influence over the merged entity.

+ CALIFORNIA TOWER TOPPING OUT SIGNALS PROGRESS IN UC DAVIS HEALTH MASTER PLAN

The California Tower is a 14-story hospital with a five-story pavilion designed to house operating rooms, an imaging center, and updated facilities for pharmacy and burn care, and will add about 334 inpatient beds. The tower is part of UC Davis Health's eight-year plan to double the campus size — the largest health-system capital expansion underway in the United States — with opening anticipated in 2030 as the project advances under Vision 2030. The initiative will expand campus space from roughly 3.6 million square feet to more than 7 million.

+ MCCARTHY HOLDINGS DETAILS 2024 EMISSIONS FOOTPRINT IN COMPREHENSIVE GHG REPORT

McCarthy Holdings, one of the largest employee-owned construction companies in the United States, released its updated 2024 GHG Emissions Report, published on 16th February 2026. Developed in partnership with Gravity, the report offers a thorough view of the company’s carbon footprint, covering Scope 1, Scope 2 and key Scope 3 emissions, with Scopes 1 and 2 undergoing independent auditing and limited assurance.The publication aligns with McCarthy’s mission to strengthen sustainability across its operations and subsidiaries and to uphold the standard of being the best builder in America. The company’s sustainability framework rests on three priorities: understanding environmental impact, acting responsibly for people and communities, and efficiently managing energy consumption throughout projects and operations.McCarthy also describes how emissions data informs broader responsible practices, including biodiversity initiatives and responsible fuel management. The organization leverages digital tools and artificial intelligence to improve project decisions and efficiency. A notable initiative is its PV recycling program for solar panels, conducted with SEIA-approved recyclers to divert waste from landfills and advance a circular economy.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ BALFOUR BEATTY [3 News That Matters]

+ TRENT JOHNSON NAMED VICE PRESIDENT OF AVIATION OPERATIONS FOR BALFOUR BEATTY IN THE CAROLINAS

Balfour Beatty promoted Trent Johnson to Vice President of operations for the Carolinas, effective Apr. 1, 2026. In the newly expanded role, he will lead and grow the company's aviation practice across North and South Carolina, leveraging nearly two decades in the construction industry. Johnson brings 12-and-a-half years with Balfour Beatty and a total of 19 years in the field. He has delivered 22 aviation projects, including targeted renovations and multi-phase programmes, with notable work at Raleigh-Durham International Airport and Albert J. Ellis Airport, helping to solidify the company's aviation footprint in the region. In the new role, he will oversee the aviation segment across the Carolinas, continuing to support existing clients while pursuing opportunities at additional regional and international airports. His focus will include guiding project teams through active airport environments, prioritising safety, operational continuity, and stakeholder coordination. Jeff Sandeen, President of Balfour Beatty in the Carolinas, lauded Johnson for building a strong reputation as a trusted leader and client partner, and said his leadership will drive the next phase of growth for the company's aviation portfolio. Johnson holds a degree in Construction Engineering Management from North Carolina State University and has been active in the community, notably with the Make-A-Wish Foundation.

+ MAINMARK UK SECURES ROLE ON BALFOUR BEATTY CP7 FRAMEWORK TO SUPPORT NETWORK RAIL

Mainmark UK has been awarded a place on the Balfour Beatty plc CP7 Framework, positioning the geotechnical contractor to support Network Rail’s CP7 investment programme running from 1st April 2024 to 31st March 2029, with an overall envelope of around £43-45 billion across Great Britain.CP7 is designed to maintain, renew, and modernise the railway, delivering a safer, more reliable, and more sustainable network. Investment priorities include infrastructure maintenance and renewals, digital signalling and technology, safety improvements, and initiatives to improve both freight and passenger performance.Within the CP7 Framework, contractors will undertake a mix of activities such as low complexity emergency callouts, planned preventative maintenance, and small scale civil engineering schemes. Mainmark UK will take on geotechnical response works, bridge repairs, structural integrity projects, platform refurbishments, and drainage clearance, among other tasks.Tom Kavanagh, General Manager at Mainmark UK, commented, "We’re looking forward to collaborating with Balfour Beatty to help deliver critical infrastructure works across the network during the CP7 period. Being selected to support Balfour Beatty on this framework is a fantastic opportunity for our team and aligns perfectly with our growth plans."Mainmark is renowned for its Teretek resin injection technology, which stabilises weak ground and fills subsurface voids with minimal disruption. As heavier freight loads and severe weather stress railway assets, Teretek helps preserve structural integrity and asset resilience. The CP7 appointment positions Mainmark to contribute to a more resilient UK rail network.

+ BALFOUR BEATTY POSTS 9% EPS RISE AS UK-US PROJECTS SUPPORT REVENUE GROWTH

Balfour Beatty PLC posted strong growth, powered by its construction arm, notably power-transmission work. The group’s market value has climbed about 66% over the past year as it rides a record order book, while underlying earnings per share rose 9% to 47.6 pence. On 11 March 2026, the group said top-line growth was supported by UK demand for power transmission and a surge in US building activity. Operating profits in the support services segment jumped a third to £122m, and the underlying margin rose 80 basis points to 8.5%. The UK construction business also exceeded its margin targets ahead of schedule. In the US, rising building volumes helped the group grow, though it faced cost overruns on a Civils project. The company maintains a dual-market strategy designed to sustain profitability amid these challenges. Analysts remain upbeat about the outlook, noting the large and expanding order book amid ongoing infrastructure investment. Balfour Beatty aims to improve margins and deliver projects on time, reinforcing its position in both the UK and US markets. Looking ahead, the company sees opportunity in expanding power grids and broader construction demand, while remaining mindful of rising costs and project-management challenges that could affect near-term performance.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ HENSEL PHELPS CONSTRUCTION CO [3 News That Matters]

+ HENSEL PHELPS SIGNS MULTI-YEAR DEAL WITH TRACK3D TO STANDARDIZE CONSTRUCTION PROGRESS MONITORING ACROSS PORTFOLIO

Track3D and Hensel Phelps have signed a multi-year agreement to roll out Track3D's Reality Intelligence platform across more than 200 active projects, elevating a pilot into a portfolio-wide deployment as of Apr. 1, 2026. The deal underscores Hensel Phelps' commitment to leveraging AI-driven construction data across its operations.The arrangement envisions standardizing Track3D for portfolio-wide progress monitoring, enabling earlier identification of on-site issues and reducing delays on complex projects, while improving schedule reliability and accountability across diverse sites.A San Francisco International Airport case study showcased the platform's ability to move teams from reactive problem-solving to proactive decision-making through 360-degree site walks and active involvement by existing field personnel, minimizing workflow disruption.In its March update, Track3D introduced an admin dashboard, Groups functionality and capture-free progress tracking directly on floorplans, designed to give major contractors centralized visibility and easier user management.The company is expanding its market approach by targeting specialty contractors, with plans to participate in the AWCI Convention & Expo 2026 to demonstrate automation of production tracking and reduction of manual reporting for drywall and ceiling professionals. Taken together, the developments reinforce Track3D's competitive position and lay groundwork for future growth.

+ MICAH (PONO) CHOY NAMED PROJECT SUPERINTENDENT FOR HAWAII REGIONAL KITCHEN AT HENSEL PHELPS

Micah (Pono) Choy has been promoted to project superintendent at Hensel Phelps, overseeing the Hawaii Department of Education Regional Kitchen – North Central Oahu project in Wahiawa. As of 1st April 2026, the promotion marks a milestone in his career with the firm, which he joined in 2018.Choy's experience spans aviation, wastewater, and federal market projects in Hawaii and Guam, where he has helped deliver complex assignments from planning to execution.Choy's collaborative leadership has allowed him to build strong partnerships across teams and consistently drive project outcomes. Hensel Phelps regards this promotion as part of its ongoing effort to develop talent from within; the Regional Kitchen project is essential for upgrading Hawaii's educational facilities and will benefit from his leadership.

+ HENSEL PHELPS ADOPTS TRACK3D REALITY INTELLIGENCE FOR PORTFOLIO-WIDE PROGRESS MONITORING

As of 25th March 2026, Track3D, an AI-driven construction technology company, announced a multi-year enterprise agreement with Hensel Phelps that expands from a pilot to deployment on more than 200 active projects.Hensel Phelps plans to standardize Track3D’s Reality Intelligence platform across its portfolio for progress monitoring, signaling confidence in the technology’s scalability across diverse project types. The extensive rollout is expected to deepen recurring revenue, strengthen customer loyalty, and create a high-profile reference account that could attract additional large contractor deals.In a notable application, Track3D pointed to a San Francisco International Airport case study where Hensel Phelps used the platform to shift from reactive to proactive decision-making, improving progress tracking and coordination across trades without major workflow changes.On the development front, Track3D disclosed a March release to boost enterprise oversight and workflow efficiency, including an admin dashboard and Groups functionality to centralize portfolio visibility and streamline user management, plus capture-free progress tracking on floorplans, enhancing operational flexibility for enterprise users.The company is also sharpening its go-to-market by targeting specialty contractors and will participate in the AWCI Convention & Expo 2026, signaling momentum in the industry as it aims to automate production tracking and protect margins. Together with the Hensel Phelps deal and the SFO case study, the March upgrades mark meaningful commercial traction for Track3D.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CLAYCO CONSTRUCTION COMPANY [3 News That Matters]

+ CLAYCO UNVEILS POWER AND ENERGY UNIT AIMING FOR 1,000 JOBS BY 2027

Chicago-based contractor Clayco has formed a new division dedicated to large solar and storage projects across the country. Unveiled on 4th March 2026, the unit, Clayco Power and Energy, targets almost 1,000 U.S. jobs by 2027 and expects roughly $300 million in annual revenue.Clayco Power and Energy will provide design, engineering, construction, and procurement for solar and battery storage systems. It is currently developing a 350-acre solar-plus-storage project in Manteno, Illinois, about an hour south of Chicago, with construction due to start this June.The move aligns with a projected rise in electricity demand of 15%–20% by 2030, driven by data centers, manufacturing, and the shift to electrified transportation. Notably, last year saw solar plus storage accounting for the majority of new capacity, underscoring the market tailwinds behind solar deployment.

+ CLAYCO PARTNERS WITH DEEP ATOMIC ON U.S. NUCLEAR-POWERED AI DATA CENTER AT IDAHO NATIONAL LABORATORY

Clayco, a leading design-build firm specializing in complex mission-critical facilities, has announced its collaboration with Swiss-American energy company Deep Atomic to support submissions to the U.S. Department of Energy for a nuclear-powered AI data center campus planned at Idaho National Laboratory, a project that could usher in a new era of energy-efficient computing.On 12th February 2026, Clayco said it would provide early-stage delivery planning, constructability analysis, and integrated design-build expertise as part of the consortium. The firm’s track record in delivering large-scale data centers positions it to help ensure the campus can be designed and built safely, efficiently, and at scale. Bob Clark, Executive Chairman and Founder of Clayco, stressed that confidence in project delivery is essential for a successful DOE submission.Deep Atomic’s approach blends advanced nuclear energy with AI data center infrastructure to deliver reliable, low-carbon power for next-generation computing. The partnership aims to establish a scalable, sustainable platform that could position the United States as a leader in clean-energy AI infrastructure; Deep Atomic founder and CEO William G. J. Theron said the mission is to accelerate deployment of advanced nuclear power to meet performance and sustainability needs, with Clayco guiding execution planning for the DOE submission.

+ CLARK URGES UNIFIED ACTION TO REBUILD FOUNTAIN PARK AFTER MAY 16 TORNADO

ST. LOUIS, Mo. (First Alert 4) - Nearly a year after the May 16 tornado devastated North St. Louis, Clayco CEO Bob Clark told business and civic leaders that rallying with political figures is essential to craft a unified reconstruction strategy for areas already under stress. Standing in Fountain Park, he said the recovery has been slow but the neighborhood shows potential for renewal.Clark said Clayco moved quickly after the storm, coordinating with Mayor Cara Spencer and city services along with local partners. The company’s long-term rebuilding effort includes funding and staffing community programs with Albion and Washington University to improve quality of life and keep the city moving forward. He praised residents for supporting each other in the immediate aftermath in the absence of formal plans.Looking ahead, he described Fountain Park as a beacon of hope and noted developments that were already under way before the tornado, including city programs such as the St. Louis Development Corporation and the Land Clearance for Redevelopment Authority that promote new housing and help families find safe, affordable homes. He stressed that everything starts with the civic community and a shared will to navigate recovery.Clark framed recovery as a gradual process, drawing on parallels to Hurricane Katrina and underscoring the need to start somewhere. He reiterated a vision of incremental progress: one house at a time, one block at a time, one brick at a time.Despite challenges, including more than 65% of affected property owners not applying for city programs, the city extended the deadline for its private property assistance program to February 28 to boost participation. Clark pointed to visible improvements since the tornado and cited the Delmar Divine development as evidence that teamwork can drive meaningful change.

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION [3 News That Matters]

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION TO GROW INNSBROOK FOOTPRINT WITH 9,100-SF EXPANSION BACKED BY HENRICO COUNTY

DPR Construction announced a 9,100-square-foot expansion of its Innsbrook office, supported by Henrico County incentives to attract and retain businesses. The date of this announcement is 29th April 2026.The expansion aims to accommodate a growing workforce and strengthen operational capabilities as part of the company’s regional growth strategy.The project will bring together teams and resources, promoting collaboration and innovation across the organization.Sustainable design features and modern office concepts will be integrated, aligning with DPR’s leadership in sustainable construction.Completion is expected within the next year, underscoring the company’s commitment to the region and its ongoing community initiatives.

+ JASON PAGE APPOINTED HEAD OF DPR CONSTRUCTION'S NASHVILLE SPECIAL SERVICES GROUP

The Special Services Group handles a broad range of work, from targeted improvements like parking garage stair installations to expansive interior buildouts for new office and entertainment spaces, and Page's leadership is expected to expand the group's capabilities to meet evolving client and stakeholder needs in Nashville. Beyond project leadership, Page mentors and coaches teams, sharing best practices for quick-turn, high-impact work and fostering a culture of collaboration and innovation as DPR expands its Nashville footprint.

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION SECURES GREAT PLACE TO WORK STATUS AS STAFF REPORT HIGH ENGAGEMENT

DPR Construction has secured Great Place To Work Certification, confirming that 97 percent of its employees say it is a great place to work, a figure about 40 points higher than the national average. The award, presented on 31st March 2026, signals a culture rooted in pride, belonging and opportunity across the firm’s projects.CEO George Pfeffer noted the certification reflects sentiments across the organization. "Being Certified(TradeMark) as a great place to work is especially meaningful because it comes directly from our people, who bring our culture to life," he said. The company emphasizes trust, support, and growth to help employees perform at their best.DPR’s operating model empowers staff to make decisions and share information openly, encouraging initiative and cross functional collaboration on complex tasks, new roles, and ideas beyond job descriptions. A strong emphasis on learning and development supports clear career paths aligned with individual aspirations.Great Place To Work® research aligns with these findings, highlighting a sense of pride and belonging among workers. The certification has helped DPR strengthen its reputation as an employer of choice, while external accolades from U.S. News & World Report and Newsweek have recognized DPR in more than 15 U.S. cities over the last year.Founded in 1990, DPR has evolved into a multi billion dollar contractor known for complex and sustainable projects across sectors. Its innovation posture—through virtual design and construction and self performing craft—helps explain its roughly 11,000 person workforce worldwide. More information about DPR’s culture and career opportunities is available on its official website.

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION [3 News That Matters]

+ TURNER UNVEILS NO-COST AI COACH AIMED AT IMPROVING CONSTRUCTION SITE SAFETY

Turner Construction has unveiled SafeT Coach, a free AI-powered safety tool that can be accessed on mobile devices to support on-site safety discussions, using Turner’s Environmental Health & Safety standards rather than generic online resources. On 9th May 2026, Turner underscored that the tool is available at no cost to users. The system is designed to promote a coaching approach to safety, guiding supervisors and craft professionals to talk through safer work methods rather than simply issuing rules. Its outputs are framed as potential hazards to help users apply professional judgment in real time. The tool was developed in collaboration with more than 80 stakeholders through design exercises and field pilots to ensure it fits real-world workflows. Since its pilot, SafeT Coach has recorded over 25,000 interactions among Turner staff, trade partners, and field teams. A concrete use case involved Darren Dreas, a superintendent on a higher-education lab project, who used SafeT Coach to decide whether a vertical shaft required a permit. The tool supplied a decision flow chart, a start-of-day permit checklist, and policy citations to inform a discussion with a trade partner's safety manager. Dreas noted the tool's efficiency in reducing research time and enabling quicker, informed decisions. Turner argues that because SafeT Coach is free, it could accelerate the adoption of advanced safety practices across the construction industry.

+ TURNER TO BUILD ORLANDO'S WESTCOURT, A $500 MILLION MIXED-USE PROJECT

Turner Construction Company has been selected to serve as the general contractor for Westcourt, a $500 million mixed-use development in downtown Orlando. The announcement was made on 5th May 2026, underscoring progress for a project that has faced several delays in recent years.The development will span about 8.5 acres and deliver 900,000 to 1,000,000 square feet of sports and entertainment space, including a 265-key Kimpton-branded hotel, 269 high-rise residential units, and up to 310,000 square feet of Class A office space.Additional components comprise more than 125,000 square feet of dining and entertainment, a 3,500-capacity live events venue, and 1,140 on-site parking spaces. At peak, as many as 800 workers will be on-site, with roughly 1,600 direct construction jobs expected across the project.Turner emphasized working with numerous Central Florida trade partners and noted that land-use entitlements were secured in April 2024. The Kimpton partnership was announced later that year, marking the brand’s first Orlando location. Public support includes a $2.5 million commitment from Orlando’s Community Redevelopment Agency, with potential reimbursement up to $40 million from future property tax growth.Looking ahead, a groundbreaking is targeted for the second quarter of 2026 with a late-2028 opening; Turner’s selection injects momentum into downtown Orlando’s most anticipated project.

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION OPENS WEWORK-BACKED OFFICE FACILITY IN MUMBAI'S AIROLI

Turner Construction is expanding its Indian footprint with a 46,000-square-foot WeWork India managed workspace in Airoli, Mumbai. The facility serves more than 650 professionals and opened on the 7th of May, 2026, underscoring Turner’s long-term commitment to India’s infrastructure and talent ecosystem.The office is exclusively designed for Turner and aims to align with the company’s global standards while utilizing WeWork India’s design capabilities. Jairam Panch, Chief Operating Officer of Turner Construction, described the space as more than a physical location, emphasizing collaboration, creativity, and excellence as core outcomes of the setup.Turner’s relationship with WeWork India began in 2022 with a 16-seat private office, which has evolved into an enterprise-grade managed office solution as Turner expanded in India. Airoli was chosen for its connectivity, talent access, and a growing business ecosystem, and the space features Turner s precision-focused approach integrated with WeWork India s design thinking, including collaborative spaces and modern amenities.The rise in demand for managed offices mirrors a broader shift in enterprise workspaces. Santosh Martin, Chief Revenue Officer of WeWork India, says companies increasingly view work environments as drivers of performance and culture. Turner’s move from a small office to a larger, customized workspace exemplifies a flexible model that supports growth while preserving corporate identity.WeWork India continues to expand its footprint among enterprise clients, with 76 percent of its Mumbai member base representing technology and finance, and operates across eight cities with more than 100,000 desks. Turner’s addition here demonstrates the growing importance of flexible workspaces in India’s business infrastructure.

+ PCL CONSTRUCTION ENTERPRISES, INC. [3 News That Matters]

+ OGCA AWARDS HIGHLIGHT COLLABORATIVE STRUCTURES' DOMINANCE AND PCL'S TRIO OF WINS

Collaborative Structures Limited dominated the OGCA Builder Awards, taking four honors at the Ontario General Contractors Association event held on the 17th of April, 2026. The company was recognized for work on the AIM Recycling facility in Kitchener and the renovation of Conestoga College's Indigenous Classrooms, underscoring its leadership in Ontario's construction scene.PCL Construction Enterprises, Inc. also earned recognition for three major Ontario projects: the Scotiabank Arena Venue Reimagination Phase 2, the Rogers Centre Renovation, and Limberlost Place, illustrating its broad capabilities across high-profile sectors.The OGCA Builder Awards celebrate innovation, safety performance, and overall excellence, with winning projects ranging from 5 million to over 300 million in value across buildings, infrastructure, and renovation. Scotiabank Arena Phase 2 carries a projected value between 150 million and 300 million, commissioned by MLSE, and aims to modernize fan experiences with a target completion in April 2025. The Rogers Centre Renovation, valued at over 300 million, focuses on seating bowl and player facilities upgrades. Limberlost Place marks Ontario's first institutional mass timber building, reflecting a shift toward sustainable construction. PCL collaborated with George Brown Polytechnic to advance mass timber education, while Geoff Howe, senior vice president, stressed the need to align project execution with client visions and unique challenges.

+ PCL CONSTRUCTION SECURES 17TH PLACEMENT ON FORTUNE'S 100 BEST EMPLOYERS

PCL Construction Enterprises, Inc. has been named to Fortune's 100 Best Companies to Work For for 2026, with the official announcement dated 2nd April 2026. The recognition signals PCL's ongoing emphasis on a high-trust workplace, employee engagement and satisfaction.This year marks the 17th time PCL has earned a place on the Fortune list, ranking No. 91. The designation relates to its employee-owned structure, which helps cultivate a culture where staff feel valued and motivated.Industry observers note that Fortune's list serves as a benchmark for organizational health and employee experience, and high rankings often align with stronger business performance.Earlier in 2025, PCL also ranked No. 11 on Engineering News-Record's Top 400 Contractors list, reflecting leadership in delivering complex projects across North America while maintaining a focus on people.The combination of these recognitions underscores PCL's strategy of balancing project excellence with employee well-being and satisfaction.

+ PCL CONSTRUCTION ADVANCES WATER-REUSE INITIATIVES AMID COLORADO RIVER CRISIS

On the 26th of March 2026, the Western United States confronted a mounting Colorado River crisis as drought tightens water supplies.Lake Powell sits 162 feet below full and holds only 26% of its capacity; the U.S. Bureau of Reclamation warns Mead could reach record lows within two years, forcing a reevaluation of basin-wide management.PCL Construction is advocating for larger investments in water reuse infrastructure to bolster resilience. They note that projected U.S. investments in water reuse are averaging about $47.1 billion annually over the next decade, underscoring the potential for municipalities to capture and reuse water.Adoption of reuse varies across the seven basin states, underscoring the need for scalable infrastructure and coordinated policy.As the fall 2026 deadline for water-management rules approaches, PCL points to its Drought Pipeline Program in Phoenix as a tangible model for maintaining supply for a growing city, which has seen a 12% population increase over the last decade. At the Colorado River Water Users Association Conference, industry leaders called for a multifaceted strategy to address climate change and aging infrastructure.

+ BRASFIELD & GORRIE, LLC [3 News That Matters]

+ JEFFERSON COUNTY APPROVES JOBS GRANT FOR BRASFIELD & GORRIE EXPANSION PROJECT

On 25th March 2026, the Jefferson County Commission unanimously approved a jobs grant to Brasfield & Gorrie to support a major expansion in Birmingham that includes a new office tower, an expanded parking deck, a training and event center, and a wellness facility.The project represents a total investment of $64,584,500, with $2,884,500 allocated for land, $56,100,000 for construction, and $5,600,000 for equipment, and is expected to create 80 new jobs at an hourly wage of $39.51. The county’s grant is capped at $240,000 to incentivize the growth."They’ve chosen to remain here although they are a worldwide company," Commission President Jimmie Stephens said, underscoring Brasfield & Gorrie’s commitment to Birmingham.In return, Jefferson County anticipates $8,000 to the general fund and $1,011,773 to the education fund, illustrating the potential benefit to the public purse. Stephens thanked Brasfield & Gorrie for its confidence and pledged to maintain a strong partnership as the project advances.

+ BRASFIELD & GORRIE ADVANCES BIRMINGHAM HQ EXPANSION, CREATING 80 LOCAL JOBS AND $64.5M INVESTMENT

Brasfield &Gorrie presented plans for a major expansion of its Birmingham national headquarters to the Birmingham City Council committee on 10th March 2026, signaling ongoing growth and a long-term commitment to the city.The plan calls for a new four-story office building on the headquarters campus along 7th Avenue South, totaling about 80,000 square feet. The first floor would serve as an event and training center, featuring a banquet hall, an auditorium, and multiple meeting and training rooms.The remaining three floors would provide office space to support Brasfield & Gorrie’s national operations, with room for future hiring. In addition, a fourth level would be added to the existing parking deck to free up roughly 125 more parking spaces for staff and visitors.The expansion would create about 80 new full-time jobs headquartered in Birmingham, with an average salary of approximately $82,000. Company leaders stressed that these roles will support operations nationwide while keeping the headquarters in Birmingham; they stated, "It will be jobs headquartered here in Birmingham to support our field and our operations. We’re really growing, and we’re committed to staying in Birmingham."The project carries a $64.5 million investment, and the City would provide incentives up to $80,000. The plan has cleared the council committee and will move to the full Birmingham City Council for final approval. If approved, construction could be completed by late 2027.

+ FOUSHEE TO LEAD BRASFIELD & GORRIE’S CAROLINAS GROWTH, BASED IN CHARLOTTE

Brasfield & Gorrie announced the appointment of Mike Foushee as Carolinas Region President, effective from 10th February 2026. He will operate from the Charlotte office and oversee strategy and operations for the firm’s Raleigh and Greenville offices in South Carolina.Foushee is a Greenville, S.C. native with more than two decades at Brasfield & Gorrie, rising through field and operations roles and most recently serving as regional president in Atlanta. In that role he led teams on major projects including Science Square at Georgia Tech and Omni Hotels developments.Brasfield & Gorrie chief executive Rob Blalock says Foushee’s deep understanding of both clients and the workforce makes him well suited to guide the region as it grows. The Carolinas are projected to generate about 1.2 billion in revenues in 2026, and Foushee will lead growth across commercial, healthcare and industrial sectors.He emphasizes the firm’s turnkey delivery model that covers site selection, in-house design, procurement and MEP management, enabling faster schedules and reduced risk. Brasfield & Gorrie has a strong Carolinas portfolio, including Ally Charlotte Center and Greenville's Carroll A. Campbell Jr. U.S. Courthouse. Beyond his leadership duties, Foushee is active in community organizations and workforce development initiatives, reinforcing the company’s commitment to regional growth and people development.

+ JE DUNN CONSTRUCTION GROUP [3 News That Matters]

+ JE DUNN LAUNCHES FORM OFF-SITE SOLUTIONS TO ACCELERATE CONSTRUCTION MANUFACTURING

JE Dunn Construction has launched Form Off-Site Solutions, its off-site manufacturing initiative focused on Design for Manufacturing (DfM) for complex construction components. The program, announced with a launch date of 6th April 2026, builds on the company’s century-long Kansas City heritage while extending capabilities nationwide.Form Off-Site Solutions structures its offerings into four product segments: metal assemblies, wood assemblies, building skin assemblies, and multi-trade assemblies, designed to meet the varied needs of owners, general contractors, trade partners, and original equipment manufacturers.The end-to-end service model covers estimating, design, manufacturing engineering, project management, and production, all aimed at improving installation efficiency, safety, and quality, while providing greater cost certainty and helping shorten project timelines amid labor shortages.Off-Site Manufacturing Director Nick Effenheim emphasizes collaboration: "Anything Form designs, our people can build. Our ability to collaborate with project teams and focus on getting their ideas, concepts, and design into a manufactured environment is something that sets us apart."JE Dunn President and CEO Gordon Lansford says the Form launch marks a major step in the firm’s ongoing push to drive innovation for clients as Form’s production facilities in Kansas City and Nashville position the unit to serve a national client base.

+ STEEL BEAM PLACED AS NORTHLAND CENTER REACHES TOPPING-OUT MILESTONE

KANSAS CITY, Mo. — The topping-out of the Northland Workforce Development Center was celebrated on 19th March 2026, as JE Dunn Construction placed the final structural steel beam for the 145,000-square-foot, two-story facility.Before the beam was hoisted into place, a beam-signing ceremony brought together the Platte County R-3 School District, partner districts, project stakeholders, and community members who signed the beam bearing project and partner logos. Brad Hokanson, Project Executive at JE Dunn Construction, said, 'This project is about creating opportunities and pathways for the next generation of skilled professionals in our region.'The center is being built to house programs in skilled trades, manufacturing, health sciences, and human services, expanding capacity for Northland Career Center and partner institutions while serving both high school students and adult learners seeking credentials. Planned spaces include flexible labs, classrooms, hands-on training environments, and technology-rich areas that reflect real-world workplaces.Located in the Northland region of Kansas City, the project is progressing on schedule, with substantial completion targeted in 2027 and a summer opening expected after the milestone. Executive Director Brian Noller of Platte County School District noted that state, local government, business, investor, and community foundation support have enabled a long-term investment in students and the region.JE Dunn is serving as construction manager, working in partnership with the school district and Clark & Enersen, with a focus on safety, schedule adherence, and coordination while maintaining open communication with stakeholders and minimizing disruption to nearby facilities.

+ JE DUNN REINSTATES CHRIS CHELETTE AS SENIOR VP, HEALTHCARE, TO EXPAND REGIONAL PRESENCE

JE Dunn Construction has announced the return of Chris Chelette, who will serve as the Senior Vice President – Healthcare. Chelette's reappointment comes after he spent nine years as the Senior Vice President of Facilities Services at Children’s Healthcare of Atlanta. This announcement was made on 13th June 2025. During his previous 17-year tenure with JE Dunn, Chelette played a crucial role in expanding the company's healthcare business unit. His extensive experience includes overseeing numerous high-profile projects across the United States, particularly within the non-profit and for-profit healthcare sectors. His return to JE Dunn is seen as a strategic move to enhance the company's healthcare presence in the southeastern region. In his new role, Chelette will be responsible for providing strategic leadership and operational oversight for healthcare construction projects throughout the southeast. Jeff Fuller, President of Healthcare at JE Dunn, expressed enthusiasm about Chelette's return, noting his visionary leadership and impactful experience in the healthcare field. Fuller emphasized that Chelette brings a national perspective and a proven track record that will benefit JE Dunn's future endeavors in healthcare construction. Throughout his career, Chelette has been instrumental in managing significant projects, such as the development of the North Druid Hills campus for Children’s Healthcare of Atlanta. This project, which totaled over $2 billion, featured the Center for Advanced Pediatrics, along with a state-of-the-art 446-bed replacement hospital that opened last year. Such accomplishments highlight Chelette's capability to manage complex healthcare construction initiatives effectively. As JE Dunn continues to invest in its healthcare construction division, Chelette's return is expected to drive growth and innovation in the company's project portfolio. The organization aims to leverage his expertise to enhance its service offerings and solidify its position as a leader in the healthcare construction industry.

value.Title: SWINERTON INCORPORATED - COMPREHENSIVE INDUSTRY AND PEER NEWS UPDATES

urn:contentItem:6JK7-WX83-S1DX-40BV-00000-00SWINERTON INCORPORATED - COMPREHENSIVE INDUSTRY AND PEER NEWS UPDATES2026-05-14T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:40:40ZLexisNexisSTOCKSWINERTON INCORPORATED - COMPREHENSIVE INDUSTRY AND PEER NEWS UPDATES

DASHBOARD: SWINERTON INCORPORATED

PermID4296594671Websitehttp://www.swinerton.comIndustryConstruction & EngineeringAddress5707 SOUTHWEST PARKWAY, BLDG. 1, SUITE 200 | AUSTIN, TX 78735

ACTIVITIES:

Swinerton Incorporated provides commercial construction and construction management services in the Western United States. It offers general construction, modernization and renovation, management and consulting, preconstruction, corporate, and renewable energy services. The companys projects include aviation, cultural, education, federal, gaming and entertainment, healthcare, hospitality, interiors, mission critical, mixed-use/residential, office, renewable energy, retail, and special. Swinerton Incorporated was formerly known as Swinerton & Walberg Co. The company was founded in 1888 and is based in San Francisco, California with additional offices in California, Colorado, Hawaii, Texas, New Mexico, and Washington.

+ SWINERTON INCORPORATED [3 News That Matters]

+ SAN FRANCISCO BREAKS GROUND ON INNOVATIVE FIRE TRAINING CENTER AS SWINERTON TAKES CM/GC ROLE

Swinerton Builders has been named Construction Manager/General Contractor for a $145 million San Francisco Fire Department training facility, with San Francisco Public Works as the client. The groundbreaking took place on 16th April 2026 in the city's southeastern district.The project centers on a 50,000-square-foot scenario district designed to mirror San Francisco neighborhoods, featuring settings such as sloped intersections and tight corridors that simulate post-earthquake conditions.The facility will also house emergency medical service training, onsite fire apparatus training, and Neighborhood Emergency Response Team sessions, complementing the scenario district.An Administration/Classroom Building will be designated as an Essential Services Building to allow immediate occupancy after a major earthquake and to serve as a backup emergency operations center; Swinerton highlights its long history of public safety projects in San Francisco.

+ KRISTEN BEJARANO JOINS SWINERTON TO EXPAND MISSION CRITICAL AND DATA CENTER CAPABILITIES

Apr. 8, 2026 — Swinerton has appointed Kristen Bejarano as Director of Mission Critical, adding depth to its leadership in data center infrastructure. Bejarano brings nearly 25 years of experience delivering complex mission critical work for global technology firms. She has launched and scaled a national mission critical and data center division and led Americas data center program strategy and procurement. In the new role she will lead the company's mission critical strategy, working with clients to translate technical requirements into plans that emphasize speed to market, sustainability, and energy efficiency. Her background in cooling technology and renewable energy integration is expected to help Swinerton meet rising market demand. Chany, Swinerton's Chief Revenue Officer, welcomed the appointment, highlighting the potential to strengthen client relationships and accelerate growth in the sector. Swinerton remains committed to quality and innovation and to serving projects across the United States as an employee-owned firm dating back to 1888.

+ SWINERTON ANNOUNCES LEADERSHIP TRIO FOR NORTHEAST DIVISION

Swinerton has announced leadership changes in its Northeast Division, naming Jay Quackenbush as vice president and division manager, effective 16th March 2026, with Nicholas Schifferle appointed director of client relations and Robert Murphy as project executive. The moves come as the firm intensifies its Northeast growth strategy.Quackenbush, a New Yorker, brings nearly three decades of experience leading complex construction projects and a strong grasp of New York City's building conditions and client networks that support both facility management and ground-up work.Schifferle, who joined Swinerton in 2025, offers more than 20 years of international business development experience, including managing design and construction programs across Asia and Australia and founding a San Francisco design-build firm. Murphy, who has overseen more than $650 million in commercial interiors projects, will lead multiple project teams to ensure high quality and client satisfaction, working with long-time division leaders Sloane Brown and Peter Fontana.Since its Northeast launch in 2021, the division has delivered a steady stream of office fit-outs, historic renovations and ground-up construction across all five boroughs and is expanding into education, residential and public sectors as the pipeline grows.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ BALFOUR BEATTY [3 News That Matters]

+ BALFOUR BEATTY ELEVATES TRENT JOHNSON TO LEAD CAROLINA AVIATION OPERATIONS

Balfour Beatty has promoted Trent Johnson to Vice President of operations for the Carolinas, effective Apr. 1, 2026, tasking him with leading and expanding the firm's aviation practice across North and South Carolina. With 19 years in construction and more than 12 years at Balfour Beatty, Johnson has delivered 22 aviation projects, including work at Raleigh-Durham International Airport and Albert J. Ellis Airport. In his new role, he will support existing aviation clients and broaden the regional footprint at airports throughout the Carolinas, while emphasising safety, continuity, and effective coordination with stakeholders. He earned a degree in Construction Engineering Management from North Carolina State University and participates in community initiatives such as the Make-A-Wish Foundation.

+ MAINMARK UK NAMED TO BALFOUR BEATTY CP7 FRAMEWORK, EXPANDING RAIL GEOTECHNICAL CAPABILITIES

Mainmark UK has joined the Balfour Beatty plc CP7 Framework, enabling it to support Network Rail’s CP7 programme from 1st April 2024 to 31st March 2029, with an estimated £43–45 billion allocated across Great Britain.CP7 focuses on maintaining, renewing, and modernising the rail network to deliver a safer, more reliable, and more sustainable railway, with priorities including maintenance, renewals, digital signaling, safety improvements, and enhanced freight and passenger performance.Mainmark UK will contribute through geotechnical response work, bridge repairs, structural projects, platform refurbishments, and drainage clearance, among other tasks, under low complexity calls, preventative maintenance, and small civil schemes.Tom Kavanagh, General Manager of Mainmark UK, said, "We’re looking forward to collaborating with Balfour Beatty to help deliver critical infrastructure works across the network during the CP7 period. Being selected to support Balfour Beatty on this framework is a fantastic opportunity for our team and aligns perfectly with our growth plans." Mainmark’s Teretek resin injection technology stabilises weak ground and fills voids without heavy excavation, helping maintain rail integrity as freight loads and weather events grow. The CP7 appointment signals a continued role for Mainmark in enhancing resilience and reliability of the UK rail network.

+ BALFOUR BEATTY’S DUAL-MARKET STRATEGY DELIVERS PROFITABILITY AMID RISING COSTS

Balfour Beatty PLC reported solid growth, driven by its construction division as power-transmission work and US building projects supported the top line. The group’s market value rose roughly two-thirds over the last year as it benefits from a record order book, while underlying earnings per share increased 9% to 47.6 pence. On 11 March 2026, the company said performance was aided by UK power-transmission demand and a pickup in US activity. Its support services unit lifted operating profit by a third to £122m, with the underlying margin improving by 80 basis points to 8.5%, and the UK construction arm hitting margin targets ahead of schedule. In the US, higher project volumes helped offset cost overruns on a Civils project, underscoring the benefits of Balfour Beatty’s dual-market approach. Analysts remain optimistic about its outlook as the order book grows and infrastructure investment continues.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ HENSEL PHELPS CONSTRUCTION CO [3 News That Matters]

+ TRACK3D AND HENSEL PHELPS ACCELERATE AI-DRIVEN ANALYTICS FOR LARGE-SCALE CONSTRUCTION

Track3D and Hensel Phelps announced a multi-year agreement to extend Track3D's Reality Intelligence across more than 200 active projects, moving beyond a pilot phase as of Apr. 1, 2026.The partnership aims to standardize portfolio-wide progress monitoring, enabling earlier detection of on-site issues and reducing delays on complex builds while boosting schedule reliability and oversight.A San Francisco International Airport case study demonstrated the platform's proactive decision-making through 360-degree site walks and involvement of field personnel, reinforcing its value in infrastructure work; a March update introduced an admin dashboard, Groups capability and capture-free floorplan status capture to streamline management.

+ HENSEL PHELPS ELEVATES MICAH CHOY TO LEAD SUPERINTENDENT ON REGIONAL KITCHEN PROJECT

Micah (Pono) Choy has been promoted to project superintendent at Hensel Phelps, overseeing the Hawaii Department of Education Regional Kitchen – North Central Oahu project in Wahiawa. As of 1st April 2026, Choy's promotion marks a milestone in his career with the firm, which he joined in 2018.In his tenure with Hensel Phelps, Choy has worked on notable projects in Hawaii and Guam across aviation, wastewater, and federal markets.His promotion underscores Hensel Phelps' focus on cultivating talent and positioning Choy to lead the Regional Kitchen initiative, which aims to enhance educational facilities in Hawaii.

+ TRACK3D AND HENSEL PHELPS SIGNAL SCALABLE AI IN CONSTRUCTION WITH 200-PROJECT DEPLOYMENT

As of 25th March 2026, Track3D, an AI-driven construction technology company, announced a multi-year enterprise agreement with Hensel Phelps that expands from a pilot to deployment on more than 200 active projects.Hensel Phelps plans to standardize Track3D’s Reality Intelligence platform across its portfolio for progress monitoring, signaling confidence in the technology’s scalability across diverse project types. The rollout is expected to deepen recurring revenue, strengthen customer loyalty, and create a high-profile reference account that could attract additional large contractor deals.In a notable case, Track3D highlighted a San Francisco International Airport deployment where Hensel Phelps used the platform to shift from reactive to proactive decision-making, improving progress tracking and coordination across trades without major workflow changes. The company also detailed a March product release that adds an admin dashboard and Groups for centralized portfolio visibility, plus capture-free progress tracking on floorplans for easier site documentation.Track3D is pursuing its go-to-market strategy with a focus on specialty contractors and plans to participate in the AWCI Convention & Expo 2026, signaling momentum in its growth as it shifts toward automated production tracking and enhanced margins.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ QUANTA SERVICES [3 News That Matters]

+ QUANTA SERVICES GROWS PLATFORM WITH TRI-CITY GROUP DEAL, ADDS WILSON CONSTRUCTION CO. AND BILLINGS FLYING SERVICE

Quanta Services completed the Tri-City Group acquisition announced on 6th April 2026, expanding its integrated engineering and construction offerings for electric power and renewables.The Davenport-based Tri-City Group has a long history in the Quad Cities dating to 1895 and rebranded in 2024 to reflect its diversified services. It includes Tri-City Electric and Tri-City Renewable Energy, with projects across 46 states and several countries.Lori Rochholz, Tri-City's marketing director, said the brand will remain unchanged and that the Palmer Family will continue to lead, ensuring continuity for customers and the community.Quanta CEO Duke Austin said the acquisition, together with Wilson Construction Co. and Billings Flying Service, broadens the crafts-skilled platform to deliver critical solutions for electric utility programs nationwide. The company, which employs about 69,000 people, aims to meet growing infrastructure demand.

+ QUANTA EMPHASIZES DISCIPLINE AND PARTNERSHIPS IN 2026 OUTLOOK AT JEFFERIES CONFERENCE

Quanta Services outlined its 2026 strategy at the Jefferies conference on Mar. 3, 2026, signaling optimism after a solid prior year. The leadership stressed opportunities in clean-energy deployment and infrastructure, with plans to bolster operations and broaden offerings. A key focus remains the data-center market and risk-managed partnerships with midcap utilities such as NIPSCO to deliver tailored solutions. The company reaffirmed its commitment to organic growth and disciplined capital allocation, including selective acquisitions to create long-term value.

+ QUANTA SERVICES ALIGNS WITH UTILITIES TO DELIVER LARGE-SCALE ENERGY-TRANSITION PROJECTS ACROSS NORTH AMERICA

Quanta Services has become a central infrastructure platform for utilities, renewables and grid modernization, and its work underpins North America's energy transition. Not a consumer brand, its reach spans power lines, substations, pipelines and fiber networks essential for a more electrified future.The company focuses on engineering, procurement and construction (EPC) services, enabling utilities to outsource complex projects and shrink contractor-management hurdles, a critical capability as networks are upgraded and decarbonized.It operates through three core lines—electric power infrastructure, underground utility and energy infrastructure, and communications infrastructure—allowing it to tackle high-voltage transmission, urban undergrounding and advanced communications networks, aided by LiDAR and grid digitalization.In a market with MasTec and MYR Group, Quanta differentiates through specialization and scale and its ability to deliver large-scale modernization, appealing to utilities investing in grid hardening and renewable integration; investors see a premium valuation indicating confidence in growth.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ BARTON MALOW COMPANY [3 News That Matters]

+ JOINT VENTURE OF BARTON MALOW AND MARCH-WESTIN TO LEAD MILAN PUSKAR STADIUM WEST TOWER

MORGANTOWN, W.Va. - West Virginia University's athletics department will partner with Barton Malow and March-Westin as construction managers for the Milan Puskar Stadium West Tower Project, set to begin after the 2026 football season. The joint venture pairs national and local leadership to guide the renovation.The firms will oversee the project from conception through completion, coordinating preconstruction planning, cost and schedule management, procurement and on-site activities, and serving as the primary liaison among the university, architects and engineers.The collaboration blends March-Westin’s campus experience with Barton Malow’s national stadium renovation background, aiming to minimize disruption to fans while delivering the modernization the project requires.

+ BARTON MALOW TO LEAD CONSTRUCTION OF ALUMNIFI FIELD FOR DETROIT CITY FC

Barton Malow will serve as construction manager for AlumniFi Field, Detroit City FC's 15,000-seat new home on the site of the former Southwest Detroit Hospital, with a planned spring 2027 opening.The engagement was confirmed on 28th April 2026 as the club moves forward in the USL Championship, highlighting the need for a larger venue to meet growing demand.Located between Corktown and Mexicantown, AlumniFi Field is designed as a community hub featuring a natural grass pitch, three permanent grandstands with canopies, and spaces for community activity and events, in addition to serving as a concert venue.Officials say the project will honor the hospital's legacy, including its commitment to diversity in hiring since 1973, and will reflect the character and authenticity of Detroit.

+ BARTON MALOW, SHERIDAN ANNOUNCE OUTREACH SESSION FOR NEW MACON ARENA

Barton Malow and Sheridan Construction will host an information session for subcontractors and suppliers on the New Macon Arena project, aiming to outline the project scope, schedule, and available trade bid opportunities for SLBE, MBE, WBE, and DBE firms. The event is slated for 24th March 2026, from 11:00 a.m. to 12:30 p.m. at the Macon Centreplex, 200 Coliseum Drive.Attendees will hear a brief presentation followed by a Q&A and time to network with project leads. Registration is optional but encouraged via the Barton Malow website.OSBA supports the outreach and stresses the importance of local participation; OSBA Director Charise Stephens-Merriweather notes that the size of a project does not come along often, and local businesses should have a competitive bid opportunity from the outset. Mayor Lester Miller adds that the arena is expected to be a significant economic driver for the city.The new Macon Arena will be constructed on the current Macon Coliseum site and is anticipated to cover over 230,000 square feet, with seating for about 8,500 for concerts. It will feature modern amenities and a flexible design suitable for various events, including sports, and Barton Malow and Sheridan Construction are collaborating to deliver a high-quality, community-focused project.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION [3 News That Matters]

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION ANNOUNCES 9,100-SF INNSBROOK EXPANSION UNDER COUNTY INCENTIVES

DPR Construction plans to expand its Innsbrook office by 9,100 square feet, backed by incentives from Henrico County. The date of this announcement is 29th April 2026.The expansion is designed to accommodate a growing workforce and enhance operations in the region.Officials say the project reflects confidence in the local market and will create additional job opportunities while reinforcing the firm’s sustainability commitments.The design will feature modern elements and sustainable practices, aligning with DPR’s mission to lead in sustainable construction.Completion is anticipated within the next year.

+ DPR TAPS JASON PAGE TO LEAD NASHVILLE SPECIAL SERVICES GROUP AND EXPAND INTERIOR BUILDOUTS

The SSG handles a range of work from parking garage stair installations to interior buildouts, and under Page's leadership the group aims to meet evolving client and stakeholder needs while Page mentors teams and promotes best practices for quick-turn, high-impact work.

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION ACHIEVES GREAT PLACE TO WORK CERTIFICATION WITH 97% POSITIVE EMPLOYEE FEEDBACK

DPR Construction has earned Great Place To Work Certification after 97 percent of its employees affirmed it is a great place to work, a rate about 40 points above the national average. The certification, awarded on 31st March 2026, highlights a culture built on pride, belonging and opportunity.According to CEO George Pfeffer, the certification reflects the real sentiments of the people who carry the company’s culture. "Being Certified(TradeMark) as a great place to work is especially meaningful because it comes directly from our people, who bring our culture to life," he said, underscoring an emphasis on trust, inclusion, and growth.DPR’s approach centers on empowering employees to make decisions and share information openly across projects. This openness supports initiative, whether in innovating on complex tasks, taking on new roles, or offering ideas outside formal job descriptions, with learning and development programs backing career progression.The recognition complements DPR’s broader reputation as an employer of choice and its ongoing achievements, including industry accolades and expansion across markets. DPR, founded in 1990, has grown into a multi billion dollar contractor employing over 11,000 professionals worldwide, with innovations such as virtual design and construction and self performing craft.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION [3 News That Matters]

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION ROLLS OUT COMPLIMENTARY AI TOOL TO STRENGTHEN ON-SITE SAFETY CULTURE

Turner Construction has introduced SafeT Coach, a zero-cost AI safety assistant that works on mobile to support real-time safety conversations on job sites. The AI leverages Turner’s Environmental Health & Safety standards rather than general internet content and was announced on 9th May 2026. Designed to cultivate a coaching mindset among supervisors and craft professionals, SafeT Coach presents outputs as potential hazards to help users apply professional judgment. The tool evolved with input from more than 80 stakeholders through design exercises and field pilots, and has logged over 25,000 interactions across Turner staff, trade partners, and field teams. In one example, a supervisor evaluated whether a vertical shaft was permit-required confined space by using a decision flow, permit checklist, and policy citations provided by the tool, facilitating a safer discussion with a partner's safety manager. The company says the free tool could raise safety standards industry-wide if widely adopted.

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION SECURES LEAD ROLE ON WESTCOURT DOWNTOWN ORLANDO $500M PROJECT

Turner Construction Company has been selected as general contractor for Westcourt, a $500 million mixed-use development in downtown Orlando, announced on 5th May 2026. The project has faced delays but gains renewed momentum with Turner at the helm.The development spans 8.5 acres and will provide 900,000 to 1,000,000 square feet of space, including a 265-key Kimpton hotel, 269 high-rise residential units, and up to 310,000 square feet of Class A office space.Other elements include more than 125,000 square feet of dining and entertainment, a 3,500-capacity venue for live events, and 1,140 on-site parking spaces. Peak construction will involve around 800 on-site workers, with about 1,600 direct construction jobs over the project’s life. Turner plans to partner with Central Florida trade firms to maximize local benefits.Key milestones include entitlements secured in April 2024, the Kimpton partnership announced later that year, and a $2.5 million commitment from Orlando’s Community Redevelopment Agency, potentially reimbursing up to $40 million through future property tax growth. Groundbreaking is targeted for Q2 2026, with a late-2028 opening anticipated.

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION JOINS WEWORK INDIA FOR LARGE-SCALE MUMBAI WORKSPACE

Turner Construction is expanding its India footprint with a 46,000-square-foot WeWork India managed workspace in Airoli, Mumbai, opened on the 7th of May, 2026, and serving more than 650 professionals.The office was designed exclusively for Turner, aligning with global standards while leveraging WeWork India’s design capabilities. Jairam Panch, Chief Operating Officer of Turner Construction, says the space is more than a location, fostering collaboration, creativity, and excellence.Turner first collaborated with WeWork India in 2022 with a 16-seat private office, and the Airoli site was chosen for its connectivity, talent access, and growing business ecosystem. The workspace integrates the company s precision-focused approach with WeWork India s design thinking and features collaborative spaces and modern amenities.The shift toward managed offices reflects a broader trend in enterprise workspaces. Santosh Martin, Chief Revenue Officer of WeWork India, notes that companies increasingly view work environments as drivers of performance and culture. Turner’s move from a small office to a larger, customized space exemplifies a flexible model that supports growth while preserving corporate identity.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ PCL CONSTRUCTION ENTERPRISES, INC. [3 News That Matters]

+ ONTARIO GENERAL CONTRACTORS ASSOCIATION AWARDS SPOTLIGHT COLLABORATIVE STRUCTURES AND PCL

Collaborative Structures Limited won four awards at the OGCA Builder Awards, presented during a ceremony on the 17th of April, 2026, for outstanding work on the AIM Recycling facility in Kitchener and Conestoga College's Indigenous Classrooms renovation. PCL Construction Enterprises, Inc. captured three honors for the Scotiabank Arena Venue Reimagination Phase 2, the Rogers Centre Renovation, and Limberlost Place, highlighting its role in advancing Ontario infrastructure and renovation projects.The OGCA Builder Awards recognize projects that exemplify innovation, safety, and overall excellence, with prize values spanning from 5 million to over 300 million. The Scotiabank Arena Phase 2 project is valued at 150 to 300 million and aims to enhance fan experiences, with completion planned for April 2025. The Rogers Centre Renovation, exceeding 300 million, concentrates on seating and player facilities. Limberlost Place stands as Ontario's first institutional mass timber building, reflecting a commitment to sustainable construction, with PCL collaborating with George Brown Polytechnic to promote mass timber education. Geoff Howe of PCL emphasized delivering client visions while navigating the unique challenges of each project.

+ PCL CONSTRUCTION HONORED ON FORTUNE'S 100 BEST LIST FOR 2026

PCL Construction Enterprises, Inc. has been named to Fortune's 100 Best Companies to Work For for 2026, the official announcement dated 2nd April 2026. The recognition highlights PCL's commitment to fostering a high-trust workplace and strong employee engagement.It marks the 17th time PCL has earned a spot on the list, with a No. 91 ranking for 2026. The designation is linked to the company's employee-owned structure, which helps cultivate a culture where staff feel valued and motivated.Fortune's list is widely viewed as a gauge of organizational health and employee experience, often correlating with stronger business performance.In addition, PCL’s year also featured an Engineering News-Record ranking, placing No. 11 on the Top 400 Contractors list for 2025, underscoring leadership in delivering complex projects across North America while prioritizing people.

+ PCL CONSTRUCTION LEADS INFRASTRUCTURE SOLUTIONS FOR COLORADO RIVER WATER SECURITY

On the 26th of March 2026, the Colorado River's drought conditions are tightening supplies across the West.Lake Powell is 162 feet below full and holds about 26% of its capacity; the U.S. Bureau of Reclamation warns that Lake Mead could reach record lows within two years, prompting a reassessment of basin-wide water management.PCL Construction is pushing for expanded water reuse infrastructure as a central resilience tool, aligning with a projected national average of $47.1 billion in water reuse investments over the next decade. Adoption varies among the seven basin states.With the fall 2026 expiration looming, PCL's Drought Pipeline Program in Phoenix provides a model for maintaining reliable supplies amid growth, and participants at the Colorado River Water Users Association Conference highlighted the need for a multifaceted approach.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ JE DUNN CONSTRUCTION GROUP [3 News That Matters]

+ JE DUNN DEBUTS FORM OFF-SITE SOLUTIONS TO ELEVATE PROJECT EFFICIENCY

JE Dunn Construction has unveiled Form Off-Site Solutions, a new off-site manufacturing initiative focused on Design for Manufacturing (DfM) for complex components. The launch occurred 6th April 2026, signaling the company’s intent to advance its Kansas City legacy into a wider market.Led by Nick Effenheim, the team merges skilled craftspeople with automation to produce metal assemblies, wood assemblies, building skin assemblies, and multi-trade assemblies.The service model spans estimating, design, manufacturing engineering, project management, and production, with the goal of improving installation efficiency, safety, and overall quality while helping reduce costs and shorten timelines.Effenheim notes, "Anything Form designs, our people can build. Our ability to collaborate with project teams and turn ideas into a manufactured reality is what sets Form apart." The Kansas City and Nashville facilities position Form for national work.

+ NORTHLAND WORKFORCE CENTER ADVANCES WITH TOPPING-OUT CEREMONY

KANSAS CITY, Mo. — JE Dunn Construction marked a topping-out milestone for the Northland Workforce Development Center on 19th March 2026, with the final steel beam installed on the 145,000-square-foot, two-story facility.A beam-signing ceremony drew attendees who left signatures and messages on a beam bearing project and partner logos, underscoring collaboration among the Platte County R-3 School District, partner districts, stakeholders, and the community. Project Executive Brad Hokanson commented, 'This project is about creating opportunities and pathways for the next generation of skilled professionals in our region.'The center is designed to support programs in skilled trades, manufacturing, health sciences, and human services, expanding capacity for Northland Career Center and partners to serve high school students and adult learners seeking credentials. Planned spaces include flexible labs, classrooms, hands-on training areas, and technology-rich environments that mirror real-world workplaces.The project, located in the Northland region of Kansas City, is on schedule with substantial completion targeted in 2027 and a summer opening anticipated after the milestone. JE Dunn is serving as construction manager in partnership with the district and Clark & Enersen, prioritizing safety, coordination, and ongoing communication with stakeholders to minimize disruption to nearby facilities.

+ CHELETTE RETURNS TO JE DUNN TO LEAD HEALTHCARE CONSTRUCTION GROWTH IN THE SOUTHEAST

JE Dunn Construction has announced the return of Chris Chelette, who will serve as the Senior Vice President – Healthcare. Chelette's reappointment comes after he spent nine years as the Senior Vice President of Facilities Services at Children’s Healthcare of Atlanta. This announcement was made on 13th June 2025. During his previous 17-year tenure with JE Dunn, Chelette played a crucial role in expanding the company's healthcare business unit. His extensive experience includes overseeing numerous high-profile projects across the United States, particularly within the non-profit and for-profit healthcare sectors. His return to JE Dunn is seen as a strategic move to enhance the company's healthcare presence in the southeastern region. In his new role, Chelette will be responsible for providing strategic leadership and operational oversight for healthcare construction projects throughout the southeast. Jeff Fuller, President of Healthcare at JE Dunn, expressed enthusiasm about Chelette's return, noting his visionary leadership and impactful experience in the healthcare field. Fuller emphasized that Chelette brings a national perspective and a proven track record that will benefit JE Dunn's future endeavors in healthcare construction. Throughout his career, Chelette has been instrumental in managing significant projects, such as the development of the North Druid Hills campus for Children’s Healthcare of Atlanta. This project, which totaled over $2 billion, featured the Center for Advanced Pediatrics, along with a state-of-the-art 446-bed replacement hospital that opened last year. Such accomplishments highlight Chelette's capability to manage complex healthcare construction initiatives effectively. As JE Dunn continues to invest in its healthcare construction division, Chelette's return is expected to drive growth and innovation in the company's project portfolio. The organization aims to leverage his expertise to enhance its service offerings and solidify its position as a leader in the healthcare construction industry.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ MCCARTHY HOLDINGS [3 News That Matters]

+ STRATEGIC MERGER UNITES AUDDIA AND THRAMANN AS MCCARTHY FINNEY, INC.

Both boards have unanimously approved the merger, with Auddia's independent special committee affirming the deal's fairness and supported by a fairness opinion from Houlihan Capital. Governance continuity is planned as Auddia's four directors will join the McCarthy Finney board, while Jeff Thramann and John Mahoney will continue as CEO and CFO. Thramann will also be subject to a 180-day lock-up on his McCarthy Finney shares. The deal is expected to close in the second quarter of 2026, subject to Auddia shareholder approval, SEC effectiveness of a Form S-4 filing, and Nasdaq listing requirements. McCarthy Finney aims to pursue AI applications across distributed AI data centers, healthtech value-based care, and AI-driven travel services, leveraging Auddia's technology portfolio.

+ MCCARTHY BUILDS MOMENTUM IN HEALTHCARE CONSTRUCTION WITH CALIFORNIA TOWER MILESTONE

The California Tower milestone aligns with UC Davis Health's broader Vision 2030, a plan to more than double the campus footprint. Opening is targeted for 2030, finalizing a major expansion that will deliver increased patient capacity and upgraded facilities. In addition to this project, McCarthy recently completed the Plaza West Tower at Barnes-Jewish Hospital in St. Louis in December, underscoring the firm's active role in healthcare construction even as early 2026 market indicators hint at a potential slowdown in some healthcare-project planning.

+ MCCARTHY HOLDINGS SETS INDUSTRY BENCHMARK WITH 2024 GHG EMISSIONS DISCLOSURE

McCarthy Holdings has released its updated 2024 GHG Emissions Report, issued on 16th February 2026 and developed in collaboration with Gravity. The report maps Scope 1, Scope 2, and key Scope 3 emissions, with the Scope 1 and 2 data independently audited and given limited assurance, demonstrating a commitment to transparency in the construction sector.As an employee-owned firm, McCarthy emphasizes its core values and regulatory readiness, with sustainability anchored in three priorities: fully understanding environmental impact, acting responsibly toward people and communities, and efficiently managing energy use. The company notes that its emissions data inform broader practices, including biodiversity work and responsible fuel management, while its digital tools and AI support smarter project decisions.In addition, McCarthy highlights an advanced PV recycling program for solar panels, partnering with SEIA-approved recyclers to divert waste from landfills and promote a circular economy. Through data-driven insights, the firm aims to strengthen communities and set a high standard for responsible construction.

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC [3 News That Matters]

+ CARLY REINER CACERES TAKES HELM OF HR FOR NATIONAL CAMPUS RECRUITING AT CLARK CONSTRUCTION

Carly Reiner Caceres has assumed the role of Director of Human Resources – National Campus Recruiting at Clark Construction Group, effective Apr. 29, 2026, tasked with coordinating nationwide campus initiatives to expand early-career talent pipelines for the company.Her career blends leadership in People Operations at Proper, where she guided HR strategy in a hybrid environment, with roles at MILLER as Director of Internal Operations and nearly a decade at Accenture as Talent Strategy Manager and Campus Recruiting Lead.Caceres’ experience spans full-cycle recruiting, stakeholder engagement, and employer branding, capabilities Clark Construction aims to leverage to attract top talent from universities across the United States. She has also led campus recruiting efforts at Grant Thornton LLP, strengthening internship programs and student engagement.Clark Construction, founded in 1906, emphasizes community impact and project excellence. Caceres’ background in communications and external relations from early roles at Charleston Magazine and the Hirshhorn Museum is expected to support her work in nurturing the next generation of construction professionals.

+ CLARK CONTRACTS TO DELIVER REPAIRS, MAINTENANCE AND REFURBISHMENT UNDER RENFREWSHIRE COUNCIL FRAMEWORK

Clark Contracts has been named to Renfrewshire Council's multi-trades framework to improve repair, maintenance and refurbishment services for domestic properties in the area. The appointment was announced on 11th February 2026 and runs through 2027.The framework covers a broad range of multi-trade works, enabling the council to better manage both responsive and planned maintenance across its housing stock and to deliver more timely service to residents.Clark Contracts is already active in the public sector, with commitments including Scotland Excel’s New Build Residential Framework and NHS National Services Scotland’s Minor Works Framework, and involvement with Crown Commercial Service and Scottish Procurement Alliance projects.Gordon Cunningham, managing director, noted the company’s track record of more than £200 million in refurbishment projects over three years and said the appointment strengthens the services available to Renfrewshire residents.

+ THE VILLAGES SET FOR ₱4-B MIXED-USE PROJECT AS CDC ENDORSES 30-MONTH BUILD

Clark Development Corp. has approved an amendment to PKR Corp.'s lease for a ₱4-billion mixed-use hub at The Villages, a 67-hectare master-planned sports and lifestyle destination in Clark Freeport. The amendment grants PKR a 30-month timeframe to construct the project and introduces performance safeguards to help ensure on-schedule delivery. CDC president and CEO Atty. Agnes VST Devanadera described the move as reinforcing investor confidence and the community’s integration within The Villages, while PKR Corp. president Daekwon Jung expressed gratitude for the partnership and pledged timely completion. PKR operates under Kyeryong Construction Industrial Co., Ltd., a South Korean builder with recognized strengths in safety, technology, and workplace practices. The development aligns with CDC’s strategy to accelerate investments, foster well-planned communities, and strengthen Clark as a business-friendly destination that attracts both local and international capital.

value.Title: SWINERTON INCORPORATED - COMPLETE UPDATES ON INDUSTRY AND PEER NEWS
